---
title: Setting up Git
---

Git tracks changes to source documents and sends approved work to GitHub,
where many course repositories render and publish automatically.

## Clone and update a repository

Clone using the URL stated in the repository documentation:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/au-mbg/example-course.git
cd example-course
```

Before editing, update your local copy:

```bash
git pull
```

Authentication may be configured through GitHub itself or through a
repository-provided task such as `pixi run github-auth`.

## Record an update

After editing and checking a local preview, inspect the changed files:

```bash
git status
git diff
```

Stage only files that belong to the update, commit them with a short
description, and push:

```bash
git add path/to/lesson.qmd path/to/figure.png
git commit -m "Clarify exercise instructions"
git push
```

If the repository publishes through GitHub Actions, the push usually starts a
fresh render and deployment. Check the Actions tab if the update does not
appear or the build reports a failure.

## Work in source documents

Prefer editing `.qmd` source documents rather than generated `.html`, `.pdf`,
or `.ipynb` outputs. Quarto Markdown is readable plain text, so `git diff`
shows meaningful content changes and conflicts can usually be resolved by
editing a few lines. Rendered notebooks can contain outputs, metadata, and
execution state that create noisy or disposable differences.

## Resolve a merge conflict

If another contributor changed the same source while you were working,
`git pull` may report a conflict. Use `git status` to identify the affected
files and open them in an editor. A conflict is marked like this:

<pre><code>
&lt;&lt;&lt;&lt;&lt;&lt;&lt; HEAD
Your local wording
&#61;&#61;&#61;&#61;&#61;&#61;&#61;
Wording from the remote branch
&gt;&gt;&gt;&gt;&gt;&gt;&gt; origin/main
</code></pre>

Choose or combine the intended content, remove all marker lines, preview the
result if it affects rendered material, then record the resolution:

```bash
git add path/to/lesson.qmd
git commit -m "Resolve conflict in lesson text"
git push
```

Do not resolve conflicts by editing generated output in place when an authored
`.qmd` source exists.
